name: Ministry of Finance
description: Jordanian Ministry of Finance responsible for fiscal policy, budget, and public debt management

name: Ministry of Interior
description: Responsible for internal security, civil affairs, and passports in Jordan

name: Ministry of Health
description: Oversees public health services, hospitals, and medical licensing in Jordan

name: Ministry of Education
description: Responsible for public education, curriculum, and school oversight in Jordan

name: Ministry of Higher Education
name_en: Ministry of Higher Education & Scientific Research
description: Oversees universities, higher education policy, and scientific research in Jordan

name: Ministry of Tourism and Antiquities
description: Responsible for tourism promotion and heritage site management in Jordan

name: Ministry of Trade and Industry
name_en: Ministry of Industry, Trade and Supply
description: Oversees industrial development, trade policy, and supply chain in Jordan

name: Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources
name_en: Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources
description: Responsible for energy policy, electricity, and mineral resources in Jordan

name: Ministry of Transport
description: Oversees transportation infrastructure, roads, aviation, and maritime in Jordan

name: Ministry of Agriculture
description: Responsible for agricultural development, food security, and rural affairs in Jordan

name: Ministry of Labor
description: Oversees labor affairs, employment, and worker rights in Jordan

name: Ministry of Justice
description: Responsible for the judicial system, courts, and legal affairs in Jordan

name: Ministry of Foreign Affairs
description: Manages Jordan's diplomatic relations and foreign policy

name: Ministry of Digital Economy and Entrepreneurship
name_en: Ministry of Digital Economy and Entrepreneurship
description: Drives digital transformation, e-government, and startup ecosystem in Jordan

name: Ministry of Water and Irrigation
description: Manages water resources, irrigation, and water policy in water-scarce Jordan

name: Ministry of Environment
description: Responsible for environmental protection, conservation, and sustainability in Jordan

name: Ministry of Social Development
description: Oversees social welfare, poverty reduction, and family affairs in Jordan

name: Ministry of Communication and Information Technology
name_en: Ministry of Communication and Information Technology
description: Oversees telecommunications, ICT policy, and digital infrastructure in Jordan

name: Central Bank of Jordan
description: Jordan's central bank; regulates monetary policy, banking sector, and financial stability

name: Jordan Securities Commission
description: Regulates and supervises the Jordanian capital markets and securities industry

name: Amman Stock Exchange
description: Jordan's sole stock exchange; lists Jordanian companies and facilitates equity trading

name: Greater Amman Municipality
description: Governing body of Greater Amman; manages urban planning, roads, parks, and public services for Jordan's capital

name: Aqaba Special Economic Zone Authority
description: Manages and promotes Aqaba's special economic zone; oversees investment, industrial, and tourism development in Aqaba

name: Royal Jordanian Airlines
description: Jordan's national carrier; operates routes across the Middle East, Europe, North America, and Asia from Queen Alia International Airport

name: Queen Alia International Airport
description: Jordan's main international airport; 35km south of Amman; handles 9M+ passengers/year; gateway to the Kingdom

name: Jordan Tourism Board
description: Promotes Jordan as a tourist destination globally; runs visitjordan.com and works with international travel industry

name: Jordan Investment Commission
description: Promotes and facilitates foreign and domestic investment in Jordan; provides one-stop investment services

name: National Electric Power Company
description: Jordan's national electricity transmission company; manages the power grid across all governorates

name: Water Authority of Jordan
description: Responsible for water supply, sanitation, and sewage infrastructure across Jordan

name: Jordan Customs Department
description: Manages imports, exports, customs duties, and border control in Jordan

name: Civil Status and Passports Department
description: Issues national ID cards, passports, and manages civil registry in Jordan

name: Income and Sales Tax Department
description: Collects income tax, sales tax, and manages tax compliance in Jordan

name: Arab Bank
description: Jordan-headquartered pan-Arab bank; one of the largest banks in the Middle East with presence in 30+ countries

name: Bank of Jordan
description: Major Jordanian commercial bank; offers retail, corporate, and investment banking services

name: Housing Bank for Trade and Finance
name_en: Housing Bank for Trade and Finance
description: One of Jordan's largest banks; specializes in real estate, trade finance, and retail banking

name: Jordan Ahli Bank
description: Jordanian commercial bank with extensive branch network; offers personal, corporate, and SME banking

name: Cairo Amman Bank
description: Major Jordanian bank operating across Jordan, Palestine, and Bahrain

name: Jordan Islamic Bank
description: Leading Islamic bank in Jordan; provides Sharia-compliant financial products and services

name: Safwa Islamic Bank
description: Jordanian Islamic bank offering Sharia-compliant banking products and digital services

name: Capital Bank of Jordan
description: Jordanian investment and commercial bank; specializes in corporate finance and wealth management

name: Jordan Kuwait Bank
description: Jordanian-Kuwaiti bank offering retail, corporate, and trade finance

name: Arab Jordan Investment Bank
description: Investment-focused bank serving corporate and institutional clients in Jordan

name: Zain Jordan
description: One of Jordan's three major mobile telecom operators; provides 4G/5G mobile and fixed broadband services

name: Orange Jordan
description: Major Jordanian telecom operator (subsidiary of Orange Group); offers mobile, fixed-line, and digital services

name: Umniah
description: Jordan's third mobile telecom operator; known for competitive pricing and youth-focused plans

name: Jordan Telecom Group
description: Holding company of Orange Jordan; one of the oldest telecom companies in Jordan, formerly Jordan Telecom

name: Blink Digital Bank
description: Jordan's first digital bank; offers fully mobile banking with instant account opening and zero fees

name: Reflect by Bank al Etihad
description: Jordan's mobile-first banking platform by Bank al Etihad; targeted at millennials and digital-native users

name: Jordan Post
description: Jordan's national postal service; offers mail, parcels, express delivery, and financial services

name: UNHCR Jordan
name_ar: المفوضية السامية للأمم المتحدة لشؤون اللاجئين

name: UNICEF Jordan
description: UNICEF operations in Jordan; focuses on child protection, education, water, and nutrition

name: UNDP Jordan
description: UN Development Programme in Jordan; supports sustainable development, governance, and economic recovery

name: World Food Programme Jordan
description: UN WFP operations in Jordan; provides food assistance to Syrian refugees and vulnerable Jordanians through e-vouchers

name: International Rescue Committee Jordan
description: IRC operations in Jordan; provides refugee support services including health, education, economic empowerment, and protection

name: Mercy Corps Jordan
description: Mercy Corps operations in Jordan; focuses on economic development, youth employment, and water access for refugees and host communities

name: Jordan River Foundation

name: King Hussein Foundation
description: Promotes democratic and civic values, education, and human development inspired by King Hussein's legacy

name: National Aid Fund Jordan
description: Jordan's main social safety net program; provides cash transfers to low-income Jordanian families

name: Royal Medical Services
description: Jordan Armed Forces' medical service; runs 7+ hospitals and dozens of clinics serving military personnel and civilians

name: King Hussein Cancer Center
description: Leading cancer treatment and research center in Jordan and the wider Middle East; WHO-accredited, serves regional patients

name: Jordan University Hospital
description: Teaching hospital of the University of Jordan; provides tertiary healthcare and trains medical students

name: Al-Bashir Hospital
description: Largest public hospital in Jordan; operated by the Ministry of Health; serves Amman's largest patient population

name: Istishari Hospital
description: Leading private hospital in Amman; offers advanced medical specialties, IVF, oncology, and cardiology

name: Arab Medical Center

name: Al-Khalidi Medical Center
description: Long-established private hospital in Amman; offers multi-specialty care and surgical services

name: Palestine Hospital Amman
description: Established private hospital in Amman serving Jordan and the Palestinian community

name: University of Jordan
description: Jordan's oldest and largest public university; established 1962; renowned for medicine, engineering, and sciences

name: Jordan University of Science and Technology
name_en: Jordan University of Science and Technology
description: Top Jordanian technical university in Irbid; ranked among the best in the Arab world; strong in engineering and medicine

name: Yarmouk University
description: Public university in Irbid; known for arts, humanities, and science programs

name: Hashemite University
description: Public university in Zarqa; offers diverse programs in engineering, sciences, nursing, and humanities

name: Mutah University
description: Public military-affiliated university in Karak; strong in engineering, military sciences, and social studies

name: German Jordanian University
description: Bilingual Jordanian-German university applying German academic standards; focuses on engineering, technology, business, and applied sciences

description: Amman branch of the Arab Open University; offers flexible distance-learning degrees in business, IT, and education

name: British Council Jordan
description: UK cultural relations organization in Jordan; offers English language training, IELTS testing, and UK education promotions

name: Roya TV
description: Popular Jordanian satellite TV channel; known for entertainment, news, and reality shows; major media outlet in Jordan

name: Jordan Times
description: Jordan's leading English-language daily newspaper; covers local, regional, and international news

name: Al-Ghad Newspaper
description: Major Arabic-language daily newspaper in Jordan; one of the most widely-read Jordanian publications

name: Jordan News Agency
description: Jordan's official state news agency; provides official news releases, government statements, and national coverage

name: Maktoob (Yahoo Arab World)
description: Pioneer Arab internet company founded in Amman in 1998; acquired by Yahoo in 2009 for $165M; first major Arab tech exit

name: Souq.com (Amazon)
description: Arab world's largest e-commerce platform; co-founded by Jordanian Ronaldo Mouchawar in Dubai; acquired by Amazon in 2017 for $650M

name: Jamalon
description: Arab world's largest online bookstore; founded in Amman 2010; ships to 22+ countries; offers 10M+ titles in Arabic and English

name: Tamatem Games
description: Leading Arab mobile gaming studio founded in Amman; publishes and localizes games for Arab audiences; 25M+ downloads

name: Mawdoo3
description: Largest Arabic content platform; Wikipedia-like encyclopedia with 100M+ monthly visitors; Jordan's biggest digital media company

name: Kharabeesh
description: Jordan-based Arab digital animation and content company; produces viral Arabic animated videos and social media content

name: Jeeran
description: Early Arab social networking site and blogging platform founded in Amman; one of the first Web 2.0 Arab services

name: Wamda
description: Arab startup media and investment platform based in Jordan; covers MENA entrepreneurship and provides resources for startups

name: Oasis500
description: Jordan's leading startup accelerator; backed by King Abdullah; has accelerated 200+ startups with seed funding and mentoring

name: Endeavor Jordan
description: Global high-impact entrepreneurship organization's Jordan chapter; supports scale-up entrepreneurs with networks and mentors

name: Plug and Play Jordan
description: Silicon Valley startup accelerator's Jordan hub; connects startups with global corporates and investors in Amman

name: Jordan Chamber of Commerce
description: National chamber representing Jordanian traders, importers, and exporters; advocates for business interests and trade facilitation

name: Amman Chamber of Commerce
description: Largest chamber of commerce in Jordan; represents businesses in Greater Amman; facilitates trade and business networking

name: Jordan Chamber of Industry
description: Represents industrial companies and manufacturers in Jordan; advocates for industry-friendly policies

name: Royal Scientific Society
description: Jordan's national applied research center; conducts R&D in energy, environment, engineering, and IT; established by King Hussein

name: National Information Technology Center
description: Jordan's e-government backbone; operates government IT infrastructure, cloud services, and digital portals

name: Jordan Phosphate Mines Company
description: One of the world's largest phosphate producers; publicly listed; major export earner for Jordan

name: Arab Potash Company
description: Extracts and exports potash from the Dead Sea; Jordan holds world's 5th largest potash reserves; publicly listed

name: Jordan Electricity Company
description: Distributes electricity across most of Jordan; subsidiary of NEPCO; serves residential and commercial customers

name: Hikma Pharmaceuticals
description: Global pharmaceutical company founded in Jordan (Amman, 1978); listed on London Stock Exchange; manufactures branded and generic drugs across MENA, US, and Europe

name: Jordan Pharmaceutical Manufacturing
description: Major Jordanian pharmaceutical manufacturer; produces 400+ generic medicines; exports to 70+ countries

name: Union Carbide Jordan
name_en: Arab Center for Pharmaceutical and Chemical Industries
description: One of Jordan's oldest pharmaceutical companies; produces a wide range of branded generic medicines

name: Umniah Telecom
description: Jordan's third mobile network operator known for affordable plans; subsidiary of Bahrain Telecommunications Company (BATELCO)

name: Middle East Insurance Company
description: Leading Jordanian insurance company; offers life, health, motor, and property insurance; publicly listed

name: Jordan Insurance Company
description: One of Jordan's oldest insurance companies; provides comprehensive insurance across all classes

name: Carrefour Jordan
description: French retail giant's Jordan operations; multiple hypermarket and supermarket locations across Amman and Jordan

name: Safeway Jordan
description: Premium supermarket chain in Jordan; known for international products, fresh produce, and imported goods

name: Cozmo Jordan
description: Popular Jordanian supermarket chain with multiple branches; offers a wide range of food, home, and personal care products

name: City Mall Amman
description: One of Amman's largest and most popular shopping malls; 350+ shops, restaurants, cinema, and entertainment

name: Mecca Mall Amman
description: Premier luxury shopping mall in Amman; home to international luxury brands, restaurants, and entertainment

name: Taj Mall Amman
description: Major shopping destination in Amman's Tabarbour district; features 200+ stores, food court, and family entertainment

name: Abdali Mall Amman
description: Modern shopping mall in the upscale Abdali Boulevard development; premium retail, dining, and entertainment in central Amman

name: Avenue Mall Amman
description: Jordan's largest shopping mall by area; located in Amman; 450+ stores, dining, and entertainment complex

name: Zara Jordan
description: Spanish fast-fashion giant Zara's Jordan stores; operates multiple locations in Amman's major malls

name: H&M Jordan
description: Swedish fashion retailer H&M's Jordan stores; affordable fashion for men, women, and children

name: Starbucks Jordan
description: American coffee chain operating 30+ locations across Jordan; popular for specialty coffee, pastries, and quick service

name: McDonald's Jordan
description: American fast-food chain with 50+ Jordan locations; operated by Americana Group; serves burgers, fries, and breakfast

name: KFC Jordan
description: Kentucky Fried Chicken franchise in Jordan; operated by Americana Group; 40+ locations across Jordan

name: Pizza Hut Jordan
description: American pizza chain with dine-in and delivery locations across Jordan

name: Burger King Jordan
description: Burger King franchise operating across multiple Amman locations and other Jordanian cities

name: Talabat Jordan
description: Middle East's largest food delivery platform; operates in Jordan with thousands of restaurant partners; owned by Delivery Hero

name: Careem Jordan
description: Middle East's ride-hailing super app (Uber subsidiary) operating in Jordan; offers rides, food delivery, and payments

name: Uber Jordan
description: Uber's ride-hailing service operating in Amman and other Jordanian cities; alternative to traditional taxis

name: Wakilni Jordan
description: Jordanian on-demand delivery and errand platform; connects customers with messengers for pickups, deliveries, and tasks

name: Mahali Jordan
description: Jordanian marketplace platform connecting shoppers with local businesses; supports SMEs and local entrepreneurs

name: Haseel Jordan
description: Jordan-based agritech startup connecting farmers directly to consumers; delivers fresh produce and seasonal goods

name: Aramex Jordan
description: Global logistics company founded in Amman (1982); headquartered in Dubai; provides international express delivery and logistics

name: JETT Jordan
description: Jordan's main public intercity bus service; connects Amman to Aqaba, Petra, and other cities; also operates VIP tourist tours

name: Dead Sea
description: World's lowest point (430m below sea level); ultra-saline lake on Jordan-Israel border; famous for therapeutic mud and floating; major tourist destination

name: Jerash Ruins
description: Best-preserved Roman city outside Italy; colonnaded streets, temples, theaters, and triumphal arches; major Jordan attraction

name: Ajloun Castle
description: 12th-century Islamic castle built by Saladin's general; overlooks forests of northern Jordan; strategic Crusader-era fortress

name: Dana Biosphere Reserve
description: Jordan's largest nature reserve (308 km²); four bio-geographic zones; 800+ plant species and 180+ bird species; run by RSCN

name: Wadi Mujib Reserve
description: Jordan's Grand Canyon; dramatic gorge leading to the Dead Sea; popular for siq trail, aquatic hiking, and adventure activities

name: Roman Theatre Amman
description: 2nd-century Roman theatre in downtown Amman (ancient Philadelphia); seats 6,000; still used for cultural events

name: Citadel Amman
description: Ancient hilltop citadel overlooking downtown Amman; contains Umayyad Palace, Hercules Temple, and Byzantine church ruins

name: Jordan National Gallery of Fine Arts
name_en: Jordan National Gallery of Fine Arts
description: Jordan's leading art museum; houses contemporary art from Jordan and 60+ developing nations; promotes Arab visual arts

name: Jordan Museum
description: Jordan's premier national museum; displays artifacts from prehistoric times through the Islamic era; features the Dead Sea Scrolls

name: Amman Design Week
description: Annual design festival in Amman; showcases regional design talent in architecture, products, fashion, and digital design

name: Jordan Fashion Week
description: Annual fashion event showcasing Jordanian and Arab designers; promotes the regional fashion industry

name: Jerash Festival
name_en: Jerash Festival for Culture and Arts
description: Annual festival held in the Roman ruins of Jerash; Jordan's largest cultural event; features Arab and international performers
